questions: You're someone who faces criticism head-on and takes full responsibility for your mistakes. Your manager just pointed out that the client presentation you led was disorganized and lost the account. What do you say?
You're someone who owns your part in conflicts without pointing fingers. Your partner says you forgot to pick up your kid from soccer practice. How do you respond?
You're someone who addresses problems directly without shifting blame to others. A professor calls you out for a group project that was submitted late. What do you tell them?
You're someone who takes accountability and stays focused on your own role when things go wrong. A neighbor confronts you about your dog getting into their garden again. How do you handle it?
You're someone who accepts fault gracefully without bringing up other people's mistakes. Your friend says you ruined their birthday party by starting an argument. What do you say?

You're someone who keeps the focus on yourself when receiving negative feedback. During a performance review, your boss mentions your team's sales numbers dropped under your watch. How do you respond?
You're someone who doesn't try to redirect attention when you're in the hot seat. A family member confronts you at dinner about not visiting your aging parents enough. What do you say?
You're someone who engages honestly with accusations without counter-attacking. Your roommate is upset that you ate their leftovers from the fridge. How do you respond?
You're someone who stays focused on the issue at hand when challenged. A coworker says your code review comments were harsh and demoralizing. What do you tell them?
You're someone who confronts their own shortcomings without dragging in others. Your coach tells you that you were the weakest player in last night's game. How do you react?
